titleStmt.title: Letter from V. Coffey to the Inspectors of Lunatics, 18 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: V. Coffey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from V. Coffey, resident medical superintendent at Maryborough (Portlaoise) Asylum, to the inspector of lunatics, discussing the case of Julia Conroy. Conroy was claiming compensation for an injury she received working at the asylum. Coffey claims, however, that the injury was not serious and healed within a few days. The letter includes a letter from the asylum's solicitor who also claims no compensation is warranted.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/8753/3

correspAction.placeName: Maryborough Asylum, Maryborough (Portlaoise), Queen's County (Laois), Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
District Asylum. Maryborough 18th May 1916 Gentlemen, In reply to your communication of 15th inst, I have
to inform you that Julia Conroy (nee Lodge) first entered the
service of this Asylum on June 1st 1890. She resigned on June
30th 1898, was reappointed on 12th May 1900, and resigned to
get married on 30th October 1908. The acident alluded to in her petition occurred through
falling off a stepladder some considerable time before her
resignation, and was not of a serious nature, nothing more than
a simple inflamation about the elbow Joint being apparent a
at the time. This passed away after a few days leaving no phys
physical signs of permanent injury. Nothign further was heard
by us about this case until last January, when an application
was made to the Committee for compensation respecting the
injury, before deciding which they referred the matter to
their Solicitor upon whose opinion (copy of which is enclosed)
they acted. Petition and medical certificate herewith returned. I am,
Gentlemen,
Your Obt Servant V Coffey
Resident Medical Superintendent. To/ The Inspectors of Lunatics Dublin Castle
Re claim of Julia Conroy for injuries alleged to have been
sustained by her whilst in the service of the Asylum. 27th day of March 1916 Telephone No 15
Horace Turpin Solicitor and Land Agent Maryborough District Asylum Maryborough Queen's County Dear Sir I am in receipt of your letter of the 24th inst with
enclosures herein, all of which I return as requested. In my opinion the Committee of Management are under no
obligation whatever to compensate the Claiment for alleged injuries
received, even if the facts stated by her in her application be correct.
It would be quite impossible for her legally to sustain such a claim
under the Workman's Compensation Act or any other act after such a lape
lapse of time. Apart from all other considerations your Committee would
indeed be establishing a very dangerous precedent in entertaining
such an application put forward for the first time more than seven
years after the Claimant had left the Asylum service. Furthermore I am of opinion that the L.G. Auditor would be
almost certain to surcharge your committee if it ventured to
entertain such a Claim. Yours Faithfully H. Turpin Wm C Meechan Esq D.L. Asylum Maryborough.
